SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion

Class Functions

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions
public final class Functions extends Object
Static factory methods for building DataFusion expressions.

This class provides the primary entry points for programmatic expression construction. With a static import, expression code reads like SQL: 

import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

col("age").gt(lit(18)).and(col("name").eq(lit("Alice")))

This parallels Rust DataFusion's prelude module and Spark's functions class.

See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: datafusion::prelude

  • Method Details

    • col

      public static Expr col(String name)
      Reference a column by name. 
      Expr ageCol = col("age");
      Parameters:
      name - the column name
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: col

    • col

      public static Expr col(String relation, String name)
      Reference a column with table qualification. 
      Expr col = col("orders", "id");
      Parameters:
      relation - the table name
      name - the column name
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: col

    • lit

      public static Expr lit(int value)
      Wrap an int value as a literal expression. 
      Expr threshold = lit(42);
      Parameters:
      value - the int value
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: lit

    • lit

      public static Expr lit(long value)
      Wrap a long value as a literal expression. See lit(int) for an example.
      Parameters:
      value - the long value
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: lit

    • lit

      public static Expr lit(float value)
      Wrap a float value as a literal expression. See lit(int) for an example.
      Parameters:
      value - the float value

    • avg

      public static Expr avg(Expr expr)
      AVG aggregate function. 
      Expr avgSalary = avg(col("salary"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to average

      SUM aggregate function. 
      Expr totalRevenue = sum(col("revenue"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to sum

      COUNT aggregate function. 
      Expr numRows = count(col("id"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to count

      MIN aggregate function. 
      Expr lowest = min(col("price"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to find the minimum of

      public static Expr max(Expr expr)
      MAX aggregate function. 
      Expr highest = max(col("price"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to find the maximum of
      See Also:

      public static Expr median(Expr expr)
      MEDIAN aggregate function. 
      Expr mid = median(col("score"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to compute the median of
      See Also:

      public static Expr stddev(Expr expr)
      STDDEV aggregate function. 
      Expr sd = stddev(col("measurement"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to compute standard deviation of
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: stddev

    • variance

      public static Expr variance(Expr expr)
      VARIANCE (sample variance) aggregate function. 
      Expr var = variance(col("measurement"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to compute variance of
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: var_sample

    • countDistinct

      public static Expr countDistinct(Expr expr)
      COUNT(DISTINCT expr) aggregate function. 
      Expr uniqueCount = countDistinct(col("category"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to count distinct values of
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: count_distinct

    • countAll

      public static Expr countAll()
      COUNT(*) aggregate function. 
      Expr totalRows = countAll();
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: count

    • when

      public static CaseBuilder when(Expr condition, Expr then)
      Start a CASE WHEN chain. 
      Expr result = when(col("status").eq(lit("active")), lit(1))
    .when(col("status").eq(lit("inactive")), lit(0))
    .otherwise(lit(-1));
      Parameters:
      condition - the WHEN condition
      then - the THEN expression
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: when

    • cast

      public static Expr cast(Expr expr, org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType dataType)
      Cast an expression to a different Arrow data type. 
      Expr asDouble = cast(col("value"), new ArrowType.FloatingPoint(FloatingPointPrecision.DOUBLE));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to cast
      dataType - the target Arrow data type
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: cast

    • tryCast

      public static Expr tryCast(Expr expr, org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType dataType)
      Try-cast an expression to a different Arrow data type (returns null on failure). 
      Expr safe = tryCast(col("value"), new ArrowType.Int(32, true));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to cast
      dataType - the target Arrow data type
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: try_cast

    • not

      public static Expr not(Expr expr)
      Logical NOT. 
      Expr notActive = not(col("is_active"));
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression to negate
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: not

    • createUdf

      public static ScalarUDF createUdf(String name, Volatility volatility, List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType> inputTypes, org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType outputType, ScalarUDFImpl fn)
      Creates a simple scalar UDF with fixed input and output types. 
      ScalarUDF doubleIt = createUdf("double_it", Volatility.IMMUTABLE,
    List.of(new ArrowType.Int(32, true)),
    new ArrowType.Int(32, true),
    (args, numRows, alloc) -> { return resultVector; });
ctx.registerUdf(doubleIt, allocator);
      Parameters:
      name - the function name
      volatility - the function volatility
      inputTypes - the expected input Arrow types
      outputType - the output Arrow type
      fn - the function implementation
      Returns:
      a new ScalarUDF
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: create_udf
